questions.question:
Reread the following sentence from paragraph 15:


“We visited the Lok Virsa museum, and it was a joy to celebrate our national heritage once again.”


What does Malala's use of the phrase “once again” reveal about Swat's past?

responses.content:
15

(14) Adam took us to Islamabad. It was the first time I had ever visited. Islamabad was a beautiful place with nice white bungalows and broad roads, though it has none of the natural beauty of Swat. We saw the Red Mosque where the siege had taken place, the buildings of the Parliament House and the Presidency, where Zardari now lived. General Musharraf was in exile in London.

(15) We went to shops where I bought school books and Adam bought me DVDs of American TV programs like Ugly Betty, which was about a girl with big braces and a big heart. I loved it and dreamed of one day going to New York and working on a magazine like her. We visited the Lok Virsa museum, and it was a joy to celebrate our national heritage once again. Our own museum in Swat had closed. On the steps outside an old man was selling popcorn. He was a Pashtun like us, and when my father asked if he was from Islamabad he replied, “Do you think Islamabad can ever belong to us Pashtuns?” He said he came from Mohmand, one of the tribal areas, but had to flee because of a military operation. I saw tears in my parents’ eyes.
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's highlight includes the entirety of paragraphs 14 and 15, which is far longer than any acceptable answer. The question asks for a single visual detail that helps readers picture Islamabad, but the student copied two full paragraphs. This exceeds the character limit and does not meet the requirement to select only the relevant descriptive detail. Therefore, the response is marked as incorrect.

responses.content: Adam took us to Islamabad. It was the first time I had ever visited. Islamabad was a beautiful place with nice white bungalows and broad roads, though it has none of the natural beauty of Swat. We saw the Red Mosque where the siege had taken place, the buildings of the Parliament House and the Presidency, where Zardari now lived. General Musharraf was in exile in London.
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's most recent highlight is: 'Adam took us to Islamabad. It was the first time I had ever visited. Islamabad was a beautiful place with nice white bungalows and broad roads, though it has none of the natural beauty of Swat. We saw the Red Mosque where the siege had taken place, the buildings of the Parliament House and the Presidency, where Zardari now lived. General Musharraf was in exile in London.' This highlight is much longer than the longest acceptable answer, which is 83 characters. The calculated character limit is 108. The student's highlight, after normalization, is 410 characters, which exceeds the limit. Therefore, the response is marked as incorrect due to excessive length, regardless of content.

We saw then the power of the media and she became a great support to us.' which has 196 characters. 1.3 × 196 = 254.8, 196 + 50 = 246. The limit is 255 characters (rounded up).

My father was almost bursting with pride at how I came across in the documentary. “Look at her,” he told Adam Ellick. “Don’t you think she is meant for the skies?” Fathers can be very embarrassing.' This has 259 characters.

Step 0C: 259 > 255, so the student's highlight exceeds the character limit. According to the rules, this is automatically incorrect, regardless of content quality.
